steps.title: Identify the total number of elements in set K
steps.content: Set K contains all positive integers less than or equal to 180, with no repetition. Therefore, K = {1, 2, 3,..., 180}, giving us a total of 180 elements.

steps.title: Count the favorable outcomes: even numbers <= 54
steps.content:
We need numbers that satisfy two conditions simultaneously: even AND <= 54. The even numbers in this range are {2, 4, 6, 8,..., 54}. To count them, divide the largest even number <= 54 by 2:
$$\text{Count} = \frac{54}{2} = 27$$
There are 27 favorable outcomes.

steps.title: Calculate the probability and identify the answer
steps.content:
Apply the probability formula:
$$P(\text{even and} \leq 54) = \frac{\text{favorable outcomes}}{\text{total outcomes}} = \frac{27}{180}$$
Reduce by dividing numerator and denominator by 9:
$$\frac{27}{180} = \frac{3}{20}$$
That confirms A is correct.
